subject: Parse yaml-encoded tosca guard policy
commit_message:
Parse yaml-encoded tosca guard policy

Added test yaml-encoded tosca guard policy (following policy/models).
Started work on junit test that ingests this guard policy.

Issue-ID: POLICY-1273
Change-Id: I92eac1c248aed815454db149162b67ac5e0205c1
Signed-off-by: Joshua Reich <jreich@research.att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-xacml-pdp-master-verify-java/78/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-xacml-pdp-master-verify-java/78

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

JUnit test are to test application code, not write code.

Patch Set 1:

I'm not sure what you mean. Test-driven development is a standard agile practice, and given that I'm familiarizing myself with the codebase as I attempt to contribute, it seemed the best way to make quick progress. I know you are under substantial pressure, so figured it would be worth trying to lend a hand on the guard stuff. I've got time starting later this morning in which I can flesh this test out and write the associated application code (e.g., in StdMetadataTranslator.java). Please let me know if you'd like me to proceed, or if you'd prefer to write yourself and have me extend that code this upcoming week. Also, if you'd like to discuss further, feel free to try my cell 516 816 2470 (if no response, just leave a message or text and I'll call you back).

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2

This test is not needed here. The parsing of these files is done in policy/models repo.
You are testing whether a file is correct. the api will take care of that for us, there are tools in place to do so.
These junit will test the functionality of the guard application.

Patch Set 1:

I'm writing this test to mirror what you've done in MonitoringPdpApplicationTest.java. The test I've added has the same name, same commented description, and is following the same format. The only salient differences I see are that (1) this test is still WIP and I've put in TODO comments for portions not yet implemented  (e.g.,  service.loadPolicies) and (2) I have asserts on lines 225 and 250 that do basic sanity checks which as you note aren't strictly necessary---are these two asserts the cause of your -1, or something else?
